description: Integrate E2E encryption into QuantChat message service and create ZK Email modules for QuantMail. Modify apps/quantchat/backend/services/message.service.ts to support E2E encrypted conversations. Create packages/auth/src/e2e/zk-email/ with keypair, encrypted-storage, client-search, and key-discovery modules.
steps: Modify apps/quantchat/backend/services/message.service.ts: Add an optional 'encryption' field to SendMessageInput. When encryption is 'e2e', the service wraps content through E2E encryption (per-recipient using SignalSession). The service stores ciphertext only. Add encryptForRecipients() helper method.

Create apps/quantchat/backend/__tests__/e2e-message.service.test.ts - Test that when encryption:'e2e' is set, the stored message content is not the original plaintext. Test the round-trip: encrypted message can be decrypted by recipient.

Create packages/auth/src/e2e/zk-email/keypair.ts - OpenPGP keypair generation using the openpgp library. Export class ZKEmailKeyPairService. Methods: generateKeyPair(name, email, passphrase), exportPublicKey(key), exportPrivateKey(key, passphrase), importPublicKey(armored), importPrivateKey(armored, passphrase). Keys stored client-side only.

Create packages/auth/src/e2e/zk-email/encrypted-storage.ts - Server stores ciphertext only. Export class EncryptedEmailStorage. Methods: encryptEmail(email, publicKey), decryptEmail(encryptedEmail, privateKey), encryptAttachment(data, publicKey), decryptAttachment(encrypted, privateKey). Uses openpgp for asymmetric encryption.

Create packages/auth/src/e2e/zk-email/client-search.ts - Encrypted searchable index with client-side decryption + filter. Export class ClientSearchIndex. Methods: buildIndex(emails, encryptionKey), search(query, encryptedIndex, decryptionKey), addToIndex(email, encryptedIndex, encryptionKey). Index encrypted with AES-256-GCM, search happens client-side after decryption.

Create packages/auth/src/e2e/zk-email/key-discovery.ts - WKD (Web Key Directory) lookup for recipient public keys. Export class KeyDiscoveryService. Methods: lookupKey(email), publishKey(email, publicKey), generateWKDUrl(email). Implements WKD URL generation per RFC draft.
Create packages/auth/src/e2e/zk-email/index.ts - barrel export for all zk-email modules
Update packages/auth/src/e2e/index.ts to also export from ./zk-email
Create packages/auth/src/e2e/__tests__/zk-email-keypair.test.ts - Test key generation produces valid OpenPGP keys, export/import roundtrip works
Create packages/auth/src/e2e/__tests__/zk-email-encrypted-storage.test.ts - Test encrypt/decrypt email roundtrip, server-stored ciphertext is not plaintext
Create packages/auth/src/e2e/__tests__/zk-email-client-search.test.ts - Test build index, search returns correct results after decryption, encrypted index is not readable without key
Create packages/auth/src/e2e/__tests__/zk-email-key-discovery.test.ts - Test WKD URL generation for emails, key publish/lookup roundtrip
acceptance_criteria: All new test files pass with NODE_OPTIONS='' npx vitest run
QuantChat E2E test: encrypted message content stored differs from plaintext input
ZK Email keypair: generate, export, import roundtrip works
ZK Email encrypted storage: encrypt->decrypt recovers original email content
ZK Email client search: encrypted index searched correctly after client-side decryption
ZK Email key discovery: WKD URL generation matches expected format
findings: All 169 tests pass (120 in packages/auth, 49 in apps/quantchat). Pre-existing TS6305 errors about packages/common dist output still present (unrelated). The openpgp v6 API uses type: 'curve25519' (not curve: 'curve25519'), and encryptKey/decryptKey accept a single passphrase string that is auto-filled for all key packets. Keys generated with passphrase are returned encrypted, so they need decryptKey before use in decrypt operations. Prettier pre-commit hook reformatted files on commit.
